text: Hello everybody, this is Firas Abu Akar. Today we are presenting a video that demonstrates bilateral uniportal VAS section from Shanghai Pulmonary Hospital.

A thirty-two years old woman was found to have two small nodules on her left lower lobe and a GGO in the superior segment of right lower lobe. A decision was made to perform a resection for all these three lesions.
A hook wire is visibly placed in the left lower lobe lesion, marked preoperatively under CT scan guidance.

The surgeon begins the operation from the left side with the patient in the right decubitus position. An intercostal uniportal incision is made at the fifth intercostal space, allowing access for resection of the two lesions in the left lower lobe using endoGIS tablets.
The surgeon examines the frozen section pathology results, which reveal atypical adenomatous hyperplasia in both lesions.
Following early testing and hemostasis, a chest drain is inserted through the same port, and the surgical wound is closed in layers.
The patient is positioned in the left decubitus position, with a chest drain placed between two pillows to prevent occlusion. A three-centimeter incision is made in the right fifth intercostal space.

The surgeon begins the resection of the superior segment of the right lower lobe by dissecting and opening the fissure above the pulmonary artery, followed by stapling the fissure using an energy stapler.
The surgeon dissects, encircles, and divides the segmental bronchus to segment six using an endo GIS device, with precise manipulation visible in the surgical field.

The surgeon dissects, clips, and divides the venous branch to segment six using an energy device. Two small arterial branches supplying segment six are identified, dissected, clipped, and divided with the same energy device.

Two endogyrary loads are used to complete the resection of the superior segment of the right lower lobe, and the segment is removed from the chest cavity. The resected tissue is identified as carcinoma in situ upon final pathology examination.
Post-operative course was uneventful and the patient was discharged home four days after the surgery.
